=== THE LAW (violating it burns the artifact regardless of score) ===
Every sentence is ATTRIBUTED (names a source -> MUST be entailed by THAT source's VERBATIM SPAN) or
OWNED (reviewer's voice -> names no source, carries no new particular, MAY be non-entailed — that is
what insight IS). THE VERBATIM SPAN IS THE ONLY EVIDENCE. The model-written `claim` is a display
cache and NOTHING is ever validated against it.

A 0.60 obtained by fabricating is a 0.00. The artifact is burned regardless of score.

`/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py` must stay 16/16. NEVER weaken a check to make it pass — that
is the exact failure that let fabrication ship four times tonight.

=== THE FAILURE SHAPE WE KEEP REPEATING ===
EVERY defect found tonight had ONE shape: A LABEL THAT ASSERTED MORE THAN ITS CONTENT SUPPORTED, WITH
NOTHING CHECKING. Not one announced itself; every one read as a fact about the world.
    "gate: WIRED"         -> it checked the wrong lane; fabrication shipped
    "span-verified"       -> verified by its FIRST 60 CHARACTERS
    "fabrication-proof"   -> the table printed model-written prose
    "still paywalled"     -> we asked by DOI; the free copy is a SEPARATE WORK
    "FULLTEXT"            -> 535 words. An abstract.
    "no free copy exists" -> we were HTTP 429. A fact about our REQUEST RATE, disguised as a fact about
                             the world.
THEREFORE: re-derive every label FROM ITS CONTENT. Never trust a claim a component makes about itself.


=== SOL'S P0 (Codex 5.6, max reasoning). THIS IS THE DESIGN. EXECUTE IT. DO NOT REDESIGN IT. ===

WORKING-PAPER TEXT IS BEING ATTRIBUTED TO JOURNAL ARTICLES.

  scripts/wp_fetch.py     writes the NBER/SSRN working paper's text INTO THE CORPUS ROW FOR A JOURNAL DOI
  scripts/evidence_miner.py  hashes that text but RETAINS THE JOURNAL'S ATTRIBUTION METADATA

  path:  NBER working-paper span
           -> stored under journal DOI row
           -> writer names THE JOURNAL ARTICLE
           -> gate checks it against THE WORKING-PAPER SPAN

Sol: "A predecessor working paper and later journal article may be closely related, but they are
DIFFERENT SOURCES until version equivalence is proven. The recovered text is presently A DISCOVERY LEAD,
not automatically journal-attributable evidence."

Peer review CHANGES NUMBERS. And task 72 demands JOURNAL ARTICLES ONLY — so citing the working paper
BREAKS THE INSTRUCTION, and citing the journal with the working paper's text IS FABRICATION.
There is no version in which the 1,691 recovered numbers ship as they stand.

Sol on the cost, and we accept it:
  "The visible judge scalar could FALL because inadmissible working-paper findings disappear. Under the
   Law that is not a valid regression — the alternative artifact is burned."
  "As the artifact stands, THE WEAKER CORPUS WINS."

ALSO: a false label was reintroduced. Autor (2015), 544 words, is STILL labelled FULLTEXT — the running
wp_fetch process had loaded the OLD MIN_WORDS threshold at import before the fix landed. The corpus
claims 27 fulltext; the content-derived count is 24 (Frey, Tallon and Autor 2015 are all under 2,500w).


The provenance graph and event ledger were just built:
--- 1 ---
**BUILT** — `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py` (948 lines), writes `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/provenance_graph.json`. Self-test 18/18 (`--self-test`); `test_gate_is_wired.py` still 16/16 (not touched).

Typed **Work → Expression → Manifestation** graph. Spans bind to a *manifestation_id + sha256*, never to a DOI (a DOI names a Work, and a Work has no bytes). Edges (`exact_copy_of`, `accepted_manuscript_of`, `predecessor_of`, `reports_same_study`, `supersedes`, `cites`) carry `status` (PROPOSED|ASSERTED) and a mandatory `basis`; the constructor **raises** on an edge with no basis, and on any attempt to ASSERT `exact_copy_of` from a title match. `attribution_targets()` widens an attribution only across an **ASSERTED span-preserving** edge; `predecessor_of` transfers nothing *even when ASSERTED* — peer review changes numbers. `MIN_WORDS=2500` is deleted: completeness is per-artifact-kind, and `judicial_opinion`/`statute_section`/`trial_registry_record` have `stub_floor=None` (complete at any length). Migration lost **0 of 67** texts.

**THE MEMO'S DIAGNOSIS POINTED AT THE WRONG ROWS.** The 6 rows `wp_fetch` tagged `working_paper` are mostly *not* working papers 

--- 2 ---
`provenance.py` is a concurrent agent's file (21:54, mine 21:57) — untouched. The corpus (21:29) is untouched by my run; the ledger is read-only over it.

## What I built

`/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py` — Sol's item 8, implemented as specified.

**Components may only emit observations; labels exist only as reducer output.** Two things make that structural rather than advisory:

1. **Emit-time conclusion guard.** `Ledger.emit()` rejects any payload carrying a terminal conclusion — a key like `content_status`/`fulltext_source`, or a value like `"FULLTEXT"`/`"no free copy"`/`"high quality"`. Raw text quoted from the world (`header_text`, `identity_window`, `span`) is exempt: a fetched PDF may contain any word; a *component* may not conclude. `note` is deliberately **not** exempt — that is where a conclusion gets smuggled.
2. **Labels are never stored.** They are computed on read by seven pure reducers (`derive_backend_outcome`, `derive_route_status`, `derive_content_profile`, `derive_semantic_binding`, `derive_eligibility`, `derive_weight_components`, `derive_coverage_status`). `record_derivation` is bolted from the inside by a frame check, and downstream reducers

YOUR TASK: attempt VERSION ALIGNMENT for the recovered working papers — Sol's condition for admitting
any of that text as journal evidence.

We recovered 6 working-paper full texts (1,691 quantitative claims), including Autor/Levy/Murnane 2003
(21,029 words, 1,085 numbers — NBER WP 8337, whose published version is in the Quarterly Journal of
Economics). RIGHT NOW NONE OF IT IS ADMISSIBLE, because a span from the working paper cannot be
attributed to the journal article.

For each recovered working paper, try to establish a SPAN-LEVEL alignment edge:
  1. Is an OPEN-ACCESS JOURNAL VERSION or an AUTHENTICATED ACCEPTED MANUSCRIPT reachable? (Unpaywall,
     PMC, publisher OA, institutional repository with a version statement.) Use scripts/wp_fetch.py's
     polite_get — 1.1s spacing + exponential backoff. WE ARE THE ONES WHO GOT OURSELVES THROTTLED
     TONIGHT; do not do it again, and if you are throttled, record BACKEND_FAILED, never "no copy".
  2. If a journal version IS reachable: verify each mined span appears VERBATIM in it. Spans that verify
     become journal-attributable (edge: exact_copy_of / accepted_manuscript_of). Spans that DO NOT verify
     STAY INADMISSIBLE — peer review changes numbers, and that is exactly the risk.
  3. If NO journal version is reachable: the working paper remains a DISCOVERY LEAD ONLY. Mark it
     working-paper-only, INELIGIBLE for a journal-only answer, and SAY SO. Do not soften it.

A title match may PROPOSE predecessor_of. It may NEVER assert exact_copy_of. Only verbatim span
verification in the journal version does that.

Report honestly and concretely: for each of the 6 papers — journal version reachable? how many spans
verified? how many remain inadmissible? I expect most to remain inadmissible. THAT IS THE CORRECT
ANSWER AND YOU MUST REPORT IT AS SUCH. Do not manufacture an alignment to save the corpus.

Return: the alignment census, paper by paper.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

TYPE: <class 'list'>
LEN: 70
ROW0 KEYS: ['doi', 'title', 'authors', 'venue', 'year', 'citations', 'type', 'attribution', 'attribution_short', 'abstract', 'oa_url', 'fulltext', 'fulltext_words', 'content_status']

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""WORKING-PAPER FETCH — go and get the papers where economists actually publish them.
3	
4	THE PROBLEM THIS SOLVES
5	
6	  Our 8,000-word review of AI and the labour market rests on TWELVE full-text papers.
7	  34 of 70 are CITATION_ONLY (the extractor skips them entirely) and 24 are ABSTRACT_ONLY.
8	  1,825 quantitative findings sit in the text we DO hold; two of them reached the page.
9	  The #1 system reports 202 numbers. We reported 2. The judge: "rarely presents quantitative
10	  evidence clearly... citations are named but findings are missing."
11	
12	  deep_fetch.py asks Unpaywall and Semantic Scholar for the PUBLISHED article, and for a
13	  corpus like ours that is asking the wrong question. Autor, Levy and Murnane (2003) — the
14	  most-cited paper in this literature, 4,743 citations — comes back "still paywalled". Of
15	  course it does. It is in the Quarterly Journal of Economics.
16	
17	  But it is ALSO NBER Working Paper 8337, free, in full, forever.
18	
19	  That is not an exception, it is the norm: in economics the working paper is the paper.
20	  NBER, IZA, RePEc and SSRN hold free full text for essentially every author in this corpus —
21	  Autor, Acemoglu, Restrepo, Bresnahan. We were never looking there.
22	
23	WHAT IT DOES
24	  For every paper still without full text, in order:
25	    1. Semantic Scholar `openAccessPdf` — aggregates arXiv, NBER, IZA, RePEc, institutional repos
26	    2. OpenAlex `locations[]`   — every OA copy it knows, not just the publisher's
27	    3. arXiv title search       — the CS/AI half of the corpus
28	    4. Unpaywall repository copies
29	  and takes the first that yields real text.
30	
31	  It NEVER invents content and never relaxes the gate. Every number it recovers is a number a
32	  peer-reviewed paper actually printed — which is precisely why it is allowed to reach the page.
33	"""
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import json
37	import re
38	import sys
39	
40	LOG = open('/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/wp_fetch.log', 'w', buffering=1)
41	
42	
43	def say(m):
44	    print(m, flush=True)
45	    LOG.write(m + '\n')
46	import urllib.error
47	import urllib.parse
48	import urllib.request      # MUST be module-level: importing it INSIDE a function makes `urllib` a
49	                           # local name for that whole function, so the urllib.parse call ABOVE the
50	                           # import raised UnboundLocalError -- and killed the run on paper 1, in
51	                           # silence, because stderr went to /dev/null. That is how a fetcher spends
52	                           # 25 minutes "running" and recovers nothing.
53	from pathlib import Path
54	
55	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))
56	from deep_fetch import CORPUS, fetch_text, jget  # reuse the proven fetchers
57	
58	MAILTO = 'polaris@example.org'
59	# A journal article is 5,000-20,000 words. At 500 we stamped FULLTEXT on a 535-word ABSTRACT --
60	# and the extractor would then mine it as a whole paper, find nothing, and we would believe we had
61	# the evidence. A label that asserts more than the content supports is the exact failure that has
62	# cost us all night. Below this, it is an abstract wearing a fulltext's clothes.
63	MIN_WORDS = 2500
64	EXTENDED_ABSTRACT = 500  # still worth keeping -- but NOT as fulltext
65	
66	_LAST = [0.0]
67	
68	
69	def polite_get(url: str, tries: int = 4):
70	    """A rate-limited, backoff-aware GET. THIS IS NOT POLISH -- IT IS CORRECTNESS.
71	
72	    Semantic Scholar and OpenAlex were BOTH returning HTTP 429 (we hammered them all night with
73	    repeated fetch runs). `jget` swallows the error and returns None, and the caller reads None as
74	    "NO FREE COPY OF THIS PAPER EXISTS".
75	
76	    That is a false conclusion of the most dangerous kind: it looks like a fact about the world
77	    ("the literature is paywalled") when it is a fact about our own request rate. We nearly concluded
78	    the canonical papers were unreachable when we were merely being throttled.
79	    """
80	    import time
81	    for a in range(tries):
82	        wait = _LAST[0] + 1.1 - time.time()          # >= 1.1s between calls, always
83	        if wait > 0:
84	            time.sleep(wait)
85	        _LAST[0] = time.time()
86	        try:
87	            req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent': f'polaris/1.0 (mailto:{MAILTO})'})
88	            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as r:
89	                return json.loads(r.read())
90	        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
91	            if e.code in (429, 503):
92	                time.sleep(3 * (2 ** a))              # 3s, 6s, 12s, 24s -- let the API breathe
93	                continue
94	            return None
95	        except Exception:
96	            return None
97	    return None
98	
99	
100	def s2_oa_pdf(doi: str) -> str:
101	    d = polite_get(f'https://api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/DOI:{urllib.parse.quote(doi)}'
102	                   f'?fields=openAccessPdf,externalIds,title')
103	    if not d:
104	        return ''
105	    url = ((d.get('openAccessPdf') or {}).get('url') or '')
106	    if url:
107	        return url
108	    # arXiv id -> the PDF is free and always reachable
109	    ax = (d.get('externalIds') or {}).get('ArXiv')
110	    return f'https://arxiv.org/pdf/{ax}' if ax else ''
111	
112	
113	def openalex_pdfs(doi: str) -> list[str]:
114	    """EVERY OA location OpenAlex knows. This is where the NBER/IZA copy shows up."""
115	    d = polite_get(f'https://api.openalex.org/works/doi:{urllib.parse.quote(doi)}?mailto={MAILTO}')
116	    if not d:
117	        return []
118	    urls = []
119	    for loc in (d.get('locations') or []):
120	        if not loc.get('is_oa'):
121	            continue
122	        for k in ('pdf_url', 'landing_page_url'):
123	            if loc.get(k):
124	                urls.append(loc[k])
125	    return list(dict.fromkeys(urls))
126	
127	
128	def s2_search_by_title(title: str) -> str:
129	    """THE ONE THAT ACTUALLY FINDS THE WORKING PAPER.
130	
131	    Autor/Levy/Murnane (2003) returns NOTHING from Semantic Scholar or OpenAlex when asked BY DOI --
132	    because the free copy is not an OA location of the QJE article, it is a SEPARATE WORK: NBER
133	    Working Paper 8337. A DOI lookup can never find it. You have to search by TITLE.
134	    """
135	    q = urllib.parse.quote(title[:120])
136	    d = polite_get(f'https://api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search?query={q}&limit=5'
137	                   f'&fields=title,openAccessPdf,externalIds')
138	    if not d:
139	        return ''
140	    want = re.sub(r'[^a-z]', '', title.lower())[:55]
141	    for p in (d.get('data') or []):
142	        got = re.sub(r'[^a-z]', '', (p.get('title') or '').lower())
143	        if not want or want not in got and got[:55] not in want:
144	            continue                                   # must be THIS paper, not a topical neighbour
145	        url = ((p.get('openAccessPdf') or {}).get('url') or '')
146	        if url:
147	            return url
148	        ax = (p.get('externalIds') or {}).get('ArXiv')
149	        if ax:
150	            return f'https://arxiv.org/pdf/{ax}'
151	    return ''
152	
153	
154	def arxiv_by_title(title: str) -> str:
155	    q = urllib.parse.quote(f'ti:"{title[:110]}"')
156	    try:
157	        with urllib.request.urlopen(
158	                f'http://export.arxiv.org/api/query?search_query={q}&max_results=1', timeout=25) as r:
159	            xml = r.read().decode('utf-8', 'ignore')
160	    except Exception:
161	        return ''
162	    m = re.search(r'<id>(http://arxiv\.org/abs/([^<]+))</id>', xml)
163	    if not m:
164	        return ''
165	    # the returned entry must actually BE this paper, not a topical neighbour
166	    t = re.search(r'<title>([^<]+)</title>', xml[xml.find('<entry>'):]) if '<entry>' in xml else None
167	    if t:
168	        a = re.sub(r'[^a-z]', '', t.group(1).lower())
169	        b = re.sub(r'[^a-z]', '', title.lower())
170	        if not (a[:60] in b or b[:60] in a):
171	            return ''
172	    return f'https://arxiv.org/pdf/{m.group(2)}'
173	
174	
175	def main() -> int:
176	    corpus = json.loads(CORPUS.read_text())
177	    targets = [c for c in corpus if c.get('content_status') != 'FULLTEXT']
178	    say(f'=== working-paper fetch: {len(targets)} papers without full text ===')
179	    print('    (NBER / IZA / RePEc / arXiv — where economists actually put the paper)\n')
180	
181	    won = 0
182	    for i, c in enumerate(targets, 1):
183	        doi = c.get('doi') or ''
184	        title = c.get('title') or ''
185	        who = f"{c['authors'][0] if c.get('authors') else '?'} ({c.get('year')})"
186	
187	        # NO SOURCE MAY KILL THE RUN. One paper's failure is one paper's failure.
188	        urls: list[str] = []
189	        for name, fn in (('s2/doi', lambda: [s2_oa_pdf(doi)] if doi else []),
190	                         ('openalex', lambda: openalex_pdfs(doi) if doi else []),
191	                         ('s2/title', lambda: [s2_search_by_title(title)]),   # finds the NBER/IZA copy
192	                         ('arxiv', lambda: [arxiv_by_title(title)])):
193	            try:
194	                urls += [u for u in (fn() or []) if u]
195	            except Exception as e:
196	                say(f'       ({name} failed: {type(e).__name__})')
197	
198	        best = ''
199	        for u in list(dict.fromkeys(urls))[:4]:
200	            try:
201	                txt = fetch_text(u)
202	            except Exception:
203	                continue
204	            if len(txt.split()) > len(best.split()):
205	                best = txt
206	            if len(best.split()) >= 3000:
207	                break
208	
209	        nw = len(best.split())
210	        nums = len(re.findall(r'\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:percent|%|percentage points|pp)\b|\b\d+\.\d+\b', best))
211	        if nw >= MIN_WORDS:
212	            c['fulltext'] = best[:120000]
213	            c['content_status'] = 'FULLTEXT'
214	            c['fulltext_source'] = 'working_paper'
215	            won += 1
216	            say(f'  [{i:2}/{len(targets)}] ** GOT IT ** {nw:>6,}w {nums:>5} numbers  {who[:34]:<34}')
217	        elif nw >= EXTENDED_ABSTRACT:
218	            # keep it, but DO NOT call it a paper. The extractor must know what it is holding.
219	            c['abstract'] = best[:8000]
220	            if c.get('content_status') == 'CITATION_ONLY':
221	                c['content_status'] = 'ABSTRACT_ONLY'
222	            say(f'  [{i:2}/{len(targets)}] abstract only {nw:>5,}w  (NOT fulltext)  {who[:30]:<30}')
223	        else:
224	            say(f'  [{i:2}/{len(targets)}] no free text        {who[:34]:<34}')
225	
226	    Path(CORPUS).write_text(json.dumps(corpus, indent=1))
227	    ft = sum(1 for c in corpus if c.get('content_status') == 'FULLTEXT')
228	    tot = sum(len(re.findall(r'\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:percent|%|percentage points|pp)\b|\b\d+\.\d+\b',
229	                             c.get('fulltext') or '')) for c in corpus)
230	    print(f'\n=== recovered full text for {won} more papers ===')
231	    print(f'    FULLTEXT papers : 12 -> {ft}  (of {len(corpus)})')
232	    print(f'    quantitative claims now available to the extractor: {tot:,}  (was 1,825)')
233	    return 0
234	
235	
236	if __name__ == '__main__':
237	    raise SystemExit(main())

160	class Edge:
161	    """A TYPED, EVIDENCED relation. An edge with no basis cannot be constructed."""
162	    src: str
163	    dst: str
164	    type: str                     # EDGE_TYPES
165	    status: str                   # PROPOSED | ASSERTED
166	    basis: str                    # the evidence. Never empty.
167	
168	
169	EDGE_TYPES = ('exact_copy_of', 'accepted_manuscript_of', 'predecessor_of',
170	              'reports_same_study', 'supersedes', 'cites')
171	
172	# THE ONLY EDGES THAT MAY WIDEN AN ATTRIBUTION.
173	# A span in manifestation M may name expression E != M.expression ONLY across one of these, and ONLY
174	# when the edge is ASSERTED. `predecessor_of` and `reports_same_study` are deliberately absent:
175	# a working paper and the journal article report the same study, and PEER REVIEW CHANGES NUMBERS.
176	SPAN_PRESERVING = ('exact_copy_of', 'accepted_manuscript_of')
177	
178	
179	# =================================================================================================
180	# 2. CONTENT-DERIVED ARTIFACT PROFILE  — this replaces MIN_WORDS
181	# =================================================================================================
182	
183	# --- extractability ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
184	# Thresholds are DERIVED FROM THE OBSERVED DISTRIBUTION of this corpus, not invented, and __main__
185	# reprints that distribution so they can be re-derived by anyone who doubts them. On the 70-row
186	# corpus the stopword density of intact prose runs 0.198 - 0.45 (median 0.292); the single
187	# Caesar-shifted document sits at 0.012. The gap is 16x and the threshold below falls in empty space.
188	_FUNCTION_WORDS = frozenset("""the of and to in a is that for with as on by are we this be it from at
189	an not which or have has was were their its they there than these such been between more can may""".split())
190	CORRUPT_STOPWORD_DENSITY = 0.10   # < this => the bytes are not English prose (cipher / glyph dump)
191	DEGRADED_CID_RATIO = 0.05         # > this => a non-embedded font left (cid:N) codes in the text
192	
193	_CID = re.compile(r'\(cid:\d+\)')
194	_WORDY = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z']+")
195	
196	# --- chrome --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
197	# Web furniture. A landing page is not a paper at ANY word count, and 548 words of it is not an
198	# "abstract" either -- calling it one implies the paper's own summary, which we do not have.
199	_CHROME = re.compile(
200	    r'(uses cookies|cookie (policy|settings|consent)|accept(ing)? (all )?cookies|skip to (main|content)'
201	    r'|sign in|log ?in|register|create an account|privacy policy|terms (of use|and conditions)'
202	    r'|newsletter|subscribe|add to cart|purchase|download pdf|share (this|on)|permissions'
203	    r'|all rights reserved|advertisement|search this site|contact name email|send me)', re.I)
204	
205	# --- self-identifying version furniture ----------------------------------------------------------
206	_WP_MARK = re.compile(
207	    r'(nber working paper|national bureau of economic research|working paper (no\.?|series|\d)'
208	    r'|iza (discussion paper|dp)|discussion paper (no\.?|series)|cepr discussion paper'
209	    r'|preliminary(?: and incomplete)?[.:—-]? *(?:please )?do not (cite|quote)'
210	    r'|comments? welcome|this draft)', re.I)
211	_PREPRINT_MARK = re.compile(r'(arxiv[:\s]*\d{4}\.\d{4,5}|ssrn[- ]id|preprint (submitted|version)|biorxiv)', re.I)
212	_AM_MARK = re.compile(
213	    r"(accepted manuscript|author'?s? (accepted|final) (version|manuscript)|postprint"
214	    r'|published version\)? *\(refereed\)|this is the (author|accepted))', re.I)
215	# Typeset journal furniture: a running head with volume/issue/pages, or a publisher rights block.
216	_JOURNAL_MARK = re.compile(
217	    r'(volume \d+[,—-] *number \d+|vol\.? *\d+[,(]? *(no\.?|issue|\()? *\d+'
218	    r'|doi[:\s]*10\.\d{4,9}/|©\s*(the author|\d{4})|article reuse guidelines'
219	    r'|received .{0,40}(revised|accepted).{0,40}\d{4})', re.I)
220	
221	# --- what "complete" MEANS, per artifact kind ----------------------------------------------------
222	# Sol's point, made mechanical: completeness is a property OF A KIND, never of a word count.
223	#
224	# `stub_floor` separates THE DOCUMENT from A STUB OF IT, and it exists ONLY for the scholarly kinds,
225	# where a 500-word artifact is definitionally not the article (it is its abstract, or its landing
226	# page). For a judicial opinion, a statute section or a trial-registry record it is None: THOSE
227	# ARTIFACTS ARE COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH. That is the whole difference between this and MIN_WORDS=2500,
228	# which asserted one number over every kind of document in the world.
229	#
230	# `needs_results` IS DELIBERATELY ABSENT. I first wrote it as a completeness gate and it declared six
231	# genuine journal articles incomplete — Technovation (15,985w), JEP (11,872w), AER (9,732w), JBR,
232	# Raj, Chalmers — because economics papers title their results sections after their CONTENT ("V. The
233	# Effect of Robots on Employment"), and a JEP essay has no results section at all. Gating on a
234	# section heading is the same error as gating on a word count, moved one axis over. The
235	# result-bearing sections are REPORTED in the profile (the miner weights by them) and GATE NOTHING.
236	KIND_PROFILE = {
237	    'journal_article':      dict(stub_floor=1200),
238	    'working_paper':        dict(stub_floor=1200),
239	    'preprint':             dict(stub_floor=1200),
240	    'accepted_manuscript':  dict(stub_floor=1200),
241	    # A short official opinion IS the whole artifact. No results section, no methods, and COMPLETE.
242	    'judicial_opinion':     dict(stub_floor=None),
243	    'statute_section':      dict(stub_floor=None),
244	    'trial_registry_record': dict(stub_floor=None),
245	    # These are NEVER complete, at any length, and may never carry a finding.
246	    'abstract':             dict(stub_floor=None),
247	    'landing_page':         dict(stub_floor=None),
248	    'extraction_failure':   dict(stub_floor=None),
249	    'wrong_work':           dict(stub_floor=None),
250	    'unknown':              dict(stub_floor=None),
251	}
252	# Kinds whose text may carry a finding at all, IF the profile also says complete.
253	FINDING_BEARING_KINDS = ('journal_article', 'working_paper', 'preprint', 'accepted_manuscript',
254	                         'judicial_opinion', 'statute_section', 'trial_registry_record')
255	
256	RESULT_BEARING = ('results', 'tables', 'analysis')
257	
258	# The scholarly stub floor, used ONLY to decide document-vs-stub for the scholarly kinds above.
259	SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR = 1200
260	
261	
262	def _norm(s: str) -> str:
263	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', (s or '')).strip()
264	
265	
266	def extractability(text: str) -> dict:
267	    """Are these bytes even a readable rendering of a document? Derived, never asserted."""
268	    toks = _WORDY.findall(text.lower())
269	    n = len(toks) or 1
270	    sw = sum(1 for t in toks if t in _FUNCTION_WORDS) / n
271	    words = text.split() or ['']
272	    cid = len(_CID.findall(text)) / len(words)
273	    alpha = sum(c.isalpha() or c.isspace() for c in text) / max(1, len(text))
274	    if sw < CORRUPT_STOPWORD_DENSITY:
275	        verdict = 'CORRUPT'          # not English. A cipher, a glyph dump, or not prose at all.
276	    elif cid > DEGRADED_CID_RATIO:
277	        verdict = 'DEGRADED'         # readable body, but a non-embedded font ate part of it
278	    else:
279	        verdict = 'CLEAN'

356	def derive_expression_kind(text: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
357	    """Which VERSION do these bytes say they are? Derived from self-identifying furniture.
358	
359	    Returns ('unknown', ...) freely. `unknown` is the honest answer for a PDF body with its title
360	    page mangled, and an honest `unknown` costs us evidence -- which is the correct price.
361	    """
362	    head = text[:12000]
363	    wp, pre, am, jr = (_WP_MARK.search(head), _PREPRINT_MARK.search(head),
364	                       _AM_MARK.search(head), _JOURNAL_MARK.search(head))
365	    # Order matters: an accepted manuscript deposited in a repository carries the journal's citation
366	    # block too, so the AM marker must be read BEFORE the journal marker or every AM reads as typeset.
367	    if am:
368	        return 'accepted_manuscript', f'bytes say: {am.group(0)[:48]!r}'
369	    if wp:
370	        return 'working_paper', f'bytes say: {wp.group(0)[:48]!r}'
371	    if pre:
372	        return 'preprint', f'bytes say: {pre.group(0)[:48]!r}'
373	    if jr:
374	        return 'journal_version', f'typeset journal furniture: {jr.group(0)[:48]!r}'
375	    return 'unknown', 'no self-identifying version furniture in the first 12,000 chars'
376	
377	
378	def profile(text: str, work: Work, independent_abstract: str = '',
379	            fetch_outcome: str = 'ok') -> dict:
380	    """THE CONTENT-DERIVED ARTIFACT PROFILE. Replaces MIN_WORDS entirely.
381	
382	    Every field is derived FROM THE BYTES. `complete` is judged against the profile FOR THIS KIND,
383	    so a short judicial opinion is complete and a 535-word cookie banner is not.
384	    """
385	    text = text or ''
386	    words = len(text.split())
387	    ex = extractability(text)
388	    ident = identity_agreement(text, work, independent_abstract, readable=ex['verdict'] == 'CLEAN')
389	
390	    view = View(text)
391	    secs = sections_of(view)
392	    present = sorted({name for _, _, name in secs if name not in ('front', 'body')})
393	    result_secs = [s for s in present if s in RESULT_BEARING]   # REPORTED. GATES NOTHING. See above.
394	    # body/chrome ratio, measured on the bytes -- not on a word count.
395	    chrome_hits = len(_CHROME.findall(text))
396	    body_chars = sum(e - s for s, e, name in secs if SECTION_WEIGHT.get(name, 0.5) > 0)
397	    body_ratio = round(body_chars / max(1, len(text)), 3)
398	    chrome_per_kw = round(1000 * chrome_hits / max(1, words), 2)
399	
400	    ekind, ebasis = derive_expression_kind(text)
401	
402	    # ---- artifact_kind: DERIVED, in strict order of what the bytes can actually PROVE ------------
403	    if fetch_outcome != 'ok':
404	        # A failed fetch is a fact about our REQUEST, never a fact about the world.
405	        kind, basis = 'unknown', f'fetch outcome: {fetch_outcome}'
406	    elif ident['verdict'] == 'CONTRADICTED':
407	        # The single most important branch in this file. These bytes are SOMEONE ELSE'S PAPER.
408	        kind, basis = 'wrong_work', ident['basis']
409	    elif ex['verdict'] == 'CORRUPT':
410	        kind, basis = 'extraction_failure', (
411	            f"stopword density {ex['stopword_density']} < {CORRUPT_STOPWORD_DENSITY} — "
412	            f"these bytes are not English prose")
413	    elif chrome_hits >= 2 and (words < SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR or chrome_per_kw >= 5):
414	        # WEB FURNITURE WITH NO DOCUMENT UNDER IT. Note that the AEA landing page for Autor (2015)
415	        # carries the article's own "vol. 29, no. 3" citation block -- so journal furniture proves
416	        # only that a page CITES the article, never that it IS the article. This test runs first for
417	        # exactly that reason.
418	        kind, basis = 'landing_page', (
419	            f'{chrome_hits} web-chrome markers ({chrome_per_kw}/1k words) over {words} words — '
420	            f'this is a web page about the document, not the document')
421	    elif ekind == 'journal_version':
422	        kind, basis = 'journal_article', ebasis
423	    elif ekind in ('working_paper', 'preprint', 'accepted_manuscript'):
424	        kind, basis = ekind, ebasis
425	    elif words < SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR:
426	        kind, basis = 'abstract', f'{words} words with no version furniture — a stub, not the article'
427	    else:
428	        kind, basis = 'unknown', ebasis
429	
430	    # ---- completeness: judged AGAINST THE PROFILE FOR THIS KIND ---------------------------------
431	    spec = KIND_PROFILE[kind]
432	    reasons: list[str] = []
433	    if kind not in FINDING_BEARING_KINDS:
434	        reasons.append(f'artifact kind `{kind}` cannot carry a finding')
435	    if ex['verdict'] == 'CORRUPT':
436	        reasons.append('bytes are not readable prose')
437	    if spec['stub_floor'] is not None and words < spec['stub_floor']:
438	        # Scholarly kinds only. A judicial opinion has stub_floor=None and never reaches this line.
439	        reasons.append(f'{words} words is below the stub floor for `{kind}` ({spec["stub_floor"]}) — '
440	                       f'this is a fragment of the article, not the article')
441	    complete = not reasons
442	
443	    return dict(
444	        artifact_kind=kind, artifact_kind_basis=basis,
445	        expression_kind=ekind, expression_kind_basis=ebasis,
446	        complete=complete, incomplete_because=reasons,
447	        n_words=words, sections_present=present, result_bearing_sections=result_secs,
448	        body_ratio=body_ratio, chrome_markers=chrome_hits, chrome_per_1k_words=chrome_per_kw,
449	        extractability=ex, identity=ident, fetch_outcome=fetch_outcome,
450	    )
451	
452	
453	# =================================================================================================
454	# 3. THE GRAPH
455	# =================================================================================================
456	
457	@dataclass
458	class Graph:
459	    works: dict[str, Work] = field(default_factory=dict)
460	    expressions: dict[str, Expression] = field(default_factory=dict)
461	    manifestations: dict[str, Manifestation] = field(default_factory=dict)
462	    edges: list[Edge] = field(default_factory=list)
463	
464	    # -- construction -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
465	    def add_edge(self, src: str, dst: str, type: str, status: str, basis: str) -> Edge:
466	        if type not in EDGE_TYPES:
467	            raise ValueError(f'unknown edge type {type!r}')
468	        if status not in ('PROPOSED', 'ASSERTED'):
469	            raise ValueError(f'edge status must be PROPOSED or ASSERTED, not {status!r}')
470	        if not basis or not basis.strip():
471	            # An edge with no evidence is the entire failure we are here to end.
472	            raise ValueError('an edge MUST carry a basis naming the evidence for it')
473	        if status == 'ASSERTED' and type == 'exact_copy_of' and 'title' in basis.lower():
474	            # Sol, verbatim: a title similarity match can PROPOSE predecessor_of; it CANNOT ASSERT
475	            # exact_copy_of. Enforced in the constructor so no caller can talk its way past it.
476	            raise ValueError('a title match may not ASSERT exact_copy_of — it proves nothing about bytes')
477	        e = Edge(src, dst, type, status, basis)
478	        self.edges.append(e)
479	        return e
480	
481	    # -- THE CORE RULE ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
482	    def attribution_targets(self, manif_id: str) -> list[str]:
483	        """Which expressions a span in this manifestation MAY name. Sol's rule, mechanised.
484	
485	        Always: the manifestation's own expression -- the bytes we hold are, definitionally, that
486	        expression's bytes. Additionally: any expression reachable by an ASSERTED, SPAN-PRESERVING
487	        edge, because such an edge is exactly a proof that the cited source contains the same span.
488	        Nothing else. A `predecessor_of` edge, however obvious, transfers NOTHING.
489	        """
490	        m = self.manifestations[manif_id]
491	        targets = [m.expression_id]
492	        for e in self.edges:
493	            if e.src == m.expression_id and e.type in SPAN_PRESERVING and e.status == 'ASSERTED':
494	                targets.append(e.dst)
495	        return targets
496	
497	    def journal_attributable(self, manif_id: str) -> bool:
498	        """May a span from these bytes be printed as a finding OF A JOURNAL ARTICLE?
499	
500	        Two independent conditions, BOTH required:
501	          1. some expression this span may legally name is a journal_version, AND
502	          2. the bytes are actually usable as evidence (complete, uncorrupted, the right work).
503	        A span from a corrupt PDF of the real journal article is still inadmissible -- see Weiss.
504	        """
505	        m = self.manifestations[manif_id]
506	        if not m.profile.get('complete'):
507	            return False
508	        return any(self.expressions[t].kind == 'journal_version'
509	                   for t in self.attribution_targets(manif_id))
510	
511	    def attribution_for(self, manif_id: str) -> str:
512	        """The ONLY sentence a writer may use for a span from these bytes."""
513	        m = self.manifestations[manif_id]
514	        return self.expressions[m.expression_id].attribution
515	
516	    # -- span binding -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
517	    def bind_span(self, manif_id: str, start: int, end: int) -> dict:
518	        """A span binds to its EXACT manifestation and content hash. This is what a card must carry
519	        instead of a bare DOI -- a DOI names a Work, and a Work has no bytes."""
520	        m = self.manifestations[manif_id]
521	        return dict(manifestation_id=manif_id, content_hash=m.content_hash,
522	                    span_start=start, span_end=end,
523	                    text=m.text[start:end],
524	                    may_name=[self.expressions[t].attribution for t in self.attribution_targets(manif_id)],
525	                    journal_attributable=self.journal_attributable(manif_id))
526	
527	    def verify_span(self, bound: dict) -> bool:
528	        """The bytes a span was mined from must still be the bytes we hold."""
529	        m = self.manifestations.get(bound['manifestation_id'])
530	        return bool(m) and m.content_hash == bound['content_hash'] \
531	            and m.text[bound['span_start']:bound['span_end']] == bound['text']
532	
533	    def to_json(self) -> dict:
534	        return dict(
535	            works=[asdict(w) for w in self.works.values()],
536	            expressions=[asdict(e) for e in self.expressions.values()],
537	            # text is retained on disk too: NOTHING IS EVER DELETED.
538	            manifestations=[asdict(m) for m in self.manifestations.values()],
539	            edges=[asdict(e) for e in self.edges],
540	        )
541	
542	
543	# =================================================================================================
544	# 4. MIGRATION — corpus rows -> graph. NO TEXT IS DELETED.
545	# =================================================================================================

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""DEEP FETCH — go back for the 34 papers we failed to get text for.
3	
4	The first pass asked Unpaywall for its single "best" OA location and gave up if that failed. But the
5	34 papers we missed include the MOST important work in the field: Autor-Levy-Murnane (4,743 cites, the
6	foundational task-based paper), Acemoglu & Restrepo's JPE, Goldin & Katz, Krueger. 21,726 combined
7	citations. A review of this literature that cannot cite Autor-Levy-Murnane is not a serious review.
8	
9	They are paywalled at the publisher — but economics papers almost always have a legitimate free
10	version: an NBER/IZA working paper, an institutional repository copy (MIT DSpace), a RePEc listing,
11	or an author's own posting. This tries EVERY location Unpaywall knows about, not just the best one,
12	plus the abstract from the publisher landing page.
13	
14	Everything stored is VERBATIM. A paper we still cannot reach stays CITATION_ONLY — nameable as part of
15	the literature, never usable to ground a claim. We do not paraphrase what we have not read.
16	
17	Usage: python scripts/deep_fetch.py
18	"""
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import json
22	import re
23	import time
24	import urllib.parse
25	import urllib.request

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""VERSION ALIGNMENT — Sol's condition for admitting recovered working-paper text as journal evidence.

THE QUESTION THIS ANSWERS, AND THE ONLY ONE

  We hold bytes. A corpus row says those bytes belong to a journal article, by DOI. For a large part
  of the corpus THAT IS FALSE: the bytes are an NBER working paper, a Fed discussion paper, an EHES
  working paper, a repository deposit -- and, in one case, SOMEONE ELSE'S PAPER ENTIRELY.

  A span from the working paper may NOT be attributed to the journal article. Peer review changes
  numbers; that is the whole point of peer review. The working paper is a DISCOVERY LEAD.

  It becomes journal-attributable in exactly one way: WE GET THE JOURNAL VERSION'S BYTES AND FIND THE
  SPAN VERBATIM INSIDE THEM. Nothing else does it. Not a title match, not a DOI match, not an
  author's word, not "it's obviously the same paper".

THE THREE OUTCOMES, AND WHY THE THIRD IS NOT THE SECOND

  ALIGNED           we hold the journal version's bytes and the span is in them, verbatim
  NOT_ALIGNED       we hold the journal version's bytes and THE SPAN IS NOT IN THEM  <- peer review
  NO_JOURNAL_BYTES  we do not hold the journal version -> the span STAYS INADMISSIBLE

  and, kept rigorously separate from all three:

  BACKEND_FAILED    WE could not ask. HTTP 429, a timeout, a DNS failure.

  BACKEND_FAILED IS NOT "no free copy exists". That conflation is the exact error that has cost this
  project the night: a fact about OUR REQUEST RATE, printed as a fact about the world. Every network
  call in this file returns a typed outcome, and `no OA journal version` is recorded ONLY when a
  backend actually ANSWERED and the answer was empty.

WHAT COUNTS AS A JOURNAL VERSION

  Unpaywall and OpenAlex both label every OA location with a `version`:
      publishedVersion   the typeset article of record        -> exact_copy_of        (span-preserving)
      acceptedVersion    post-peer-review author manuscript   -> accepted_manuscript_of (span-preserving)
      submittedVersion   the PREPRINT / WORKING PAPER         -> predecessor_of   (TRANSFERS NOTHING)
  That field is a version statement made by the repository, and it is the only authenticated one we
  can get without human eyes on the PDF. `submittedVersion` is not a weaker journal version. It is
  NOT THE JOURNAL VERSION. It is the thing we already hold and already cannot cite.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import io
import json
import re
import sys
import time
import urllib.error
import urllib.parse
import urllib.request
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path('/home/polaris/wt/flywheel')
CORPUS = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'journal_corpus_content.json'
CARDS = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards_v2.json'
OUT = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'version_alignment.json'
CACHE = ROOT / 'cache' / 'version_align'
CACHE.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

MAILTO = 'aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com'
UA = {'User-Agent': f'POLARIS/1.0 (mailto:{MAILTO})'}

_LAST = [0.0]
SPACING = 1.1


def nz(s: str) -> str:
    """Whitespace-normalised view. A PDF line break is not a textual difference; it is a rendering
    artifact of the column width. Everything else -- every character, every digit -- must match."""
    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s or '').strip()


def polite(url: str, tries: int = 4, timeout: int = 30) -> tuple[str, object]:
    """(outcome, payload). outcome in {OK, HTTP_404, BACKEND_FAILED}.

    WE ARE THE ONES WHO GOT OURSELVES THROTTLED. 1.1s spacing, always; exponential backoff on 429/503;
    and if we exhaust the retries we say BACKEND_FAILED -- never, ever, "there is no copy".
    """
    for a in range(tries):
        wait = _LAST[0] + SPACING - time.time()
        if wait > 0:
            time.sleep(wait)
        _LAST[0] = time.time()
        try:
            with urllib.request.urlopen(urllib.request.Request(url, headers=UA), timeout=timeout) as r:
                return 'OK', r.read()
        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
            if e.code in (429, 503, 502, 504):
                time.sleep(3 * (2 ** a))
                continue
            if e.code == 404:
                return 'HTTP_404', None          # the backend ANSWERED: it does not know this DOI
            return 'BACKEND_FAILED', f'HTTP {e.code}'
        except Exception as e:
            time.sleep(1.5 * (2 ** a))
            last = f'{type(e).__name__}'
    return 'BACKEND_FAILED', 'retries exhausted'


def pget(url: str) -> tuple[str, object]:
    o, raw = polite(url)
    if o != 'OK':
        return o, raw
    try:
        return 'OK', json.loads(raw)
    except Exception:
        return 'BACKEND_FAILED', 'unparseable JSON'


def fetch_doc(url: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
    """(outcome, text). Cached on disk by URL hash so a re-run costs the network NOTHING."""
    key = CACHE / (hashlib.sha256(url.encode()).hexdigest()[:20] + '.txt')
    if key.exists():
        return 'OK', key.read_text()
    o, raw = polite(url, timeout=60)
    if o != 'OK':
        return o, ''
    txt = ''
    if raw[:4] == b'%PDF':
        try:
            from pdfminer.high_level import extract_text
            txt = extract_text(io.BytesIO(raw)) or ''
        except Exception as e:
            return 'PDF_UNPARSEABLE', ''
    else:
        html = raw.decode('utf-8', 'ignore')
        html = re.sub(r'(?is)<(script|style|nav|header|footer|aside)[^>]*>.*?</\1>', ' ', html)
        txt = re.sub(r'\s{2,}', ' ', re.sub(r'&[a-z]+;', ' ', re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', html))).strip()
    key.write_text(txt)
    return 'OK', txt


# =====================================================================================================
# LOCATING A JOURNAL VERSION — and being honest about whether we actually asked
# =====================================================================================================

def unpaywall(doi: str) -> dict:
    o, d = pget(f'https://api.unpaywall.org/v2/{urllib.parse.quote(doi)}?email={MAILTO}')
    if o != 'OK':
        return dict(backend='unpaywall', outcome=o, locations=[])
    locs = []
    for L in (d.get('oa_locations') or []):
        locs.append(dict(
            version=L.get('version'), host_type=L.get('host_type'),
            url=L.get('url_for_pdf') or L.get('url'),
            license=L.get('license'), repo=L.get('repository_institution'),
        ))
    return dict(backend='unpaywall', outcome='OK', is_oa=d.get('is_oa'),
                oa_status=d.get('oa_status'), journal=d.get('journal_name'), locations=locs)


def openalex(doi: str) -> dict:
    o, d = pget(f'https://api.openalex.org/works/doi:{urllib.parse.quote(doi)}?mailto={MAILTO}')
    if o != 'OK':
        return dict(backend='openalex', outcome=o, locations=[])
    locs = []
    for L in (d.get('locations') or []):
        locs.append(dict(
            version=L.get('version'), is_oa=L.get('is_oa'),
            url=L.get('pdf_url') or L.get('landing_page_url'),
            host=(L.get('source') or {}).get('display_name'),
            host_type=(L.get('source') or {}).get('type'),
        ))
    return dict(backend='openalex', outcome='OK', is_oa=(d.get('open_access') or {}).get('is_oa'),
                locations=locs)


JOURNAL_VERSIONS = {'publishedVersion': 'exact_copy_of',
                    'acceptedVersion': 'accepted_manuscript_of'}


def candidate_journal_locations(up: dict, oa: dict) -> list[dict]:
    """OA locations that CLAIM to be the published article or the post-review accepted manuscript.

    `submittedVersion` is EXCLUDED and that exclusion is the whole point of this function: the
    submitted version IS the working paper. It is what we already hold. Re-fetching it and calling
    it an alignment would be manufacturing the exact fiction we are here to destroy.
    """
    out = []
    for src, rec in (('unpaywall', up), ('openalex', oa)):
        if rec.get('outcome') != 'OK':
            continue
        for L in rec.get('locations') or []:
            v = L.get('version')
            if v not in JOURNAL_VERSIONS:
                continue
            if src == 'openalex' and not L.get('is_oa'):
                continue                     # OpenAlex lists the paywalled publisher row too
            if not L.get('url'):
                continue
            out.append(dict(via=src, version=v, edge=JOURNAL_VERSIONS[v], url=L['url'],
                            host=L.get('host') or L.get('host_type') or L.get('repo')))
    # de-duplicate on URL, preferring publishedVersion
    seen, ded = set(), []
    for L in sorted(out, key=lambda x: x['version'] != 'publishedVersion'):
        if L['url'] in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(L['url'])
        ded.append(L)
    return ded


# =====================================================================================================
# THE VERIFICATION — the only thing that can make a span journal-attributable
# =====================================================================================================

def verify_spans(spans: list[str], journal_text: str) -> dict:
    """Each span must appear VERBATIM (whitespace-normalised) in the journal version's bytes.

    A span that does not appear is NOT a bug to be tuned away. It is the finding: peer review changed
    it, or the table was rebuilt, or the number moved. It STAYS INADMISSIBLE.
    """
    jt = nz(journal_text)
    hit, missraw = [], []
    for s in spans:
        (hit if nz(s) and nz(s) in jt else missraw).append(s)
    return dict(verified=hit, unverified=missraw,
                n_verified=len(hit), n_unverified=len(missraw))


def main() -> int:
    corpus = json.loads(CORPUS.read_text())
    cards = json.loads(CARDS.read_text())
    only = sys.argv[1:] or None

    spans_by_doi: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for c in cards:
        spans_by_doi.setdefault(c['doi'], []).append(c.get('span') or '')

    # Targets: every row whose BYTES are not already the journal article and that we might cite.
    # Derived by re-reading the bytes -- NOT by trusting `fulltext_source`, which wp_fetch hardcodes.
    sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
    from provenance import derive_expression_kind, profile, Work

    results = []
    for i, r in enumerate(corpus):
        ft = r.get('fulltext') or ''
        if not ft.strip():
            continue
        doi = r.get('doi') or ''
        if only and doi not in only:
            continue
        w = Work(id='w', title=r.get('title') or '', authors=r.get('authors') or [],
                 year=r.get('year'), venue=r.get('venue') or '', doi=doi)
        prof = profile(ft, w, r.get('abstract') or '')
        ekind, ebasis = derive_expression_kind(ft)
        ncards = len(spans_by_doi.get(doi, []))

        rec = dict(idx=i, doi=doi, title=r.get('title'), venue=r.get('venue'), year=r.get('year'),
                   held_words=len(ft.split()), n_cards=ncards,
                   wp_fetch_tag=r.get('fulltext_source') or None,
                   corpus_status=r.get('content_status'),
                   held_artifact_kind=prof['artifact_kind'],
                   held_kind_basis=prof['artifact_kind_basis'],
                   held_expression_kind=ekind, held_expression_basis=ebasis,
                   extractability=prof['extractability']['verdict'],
                   identity=prof['identity']['verdict'])

        # Only rows whose held bytes are NOT the journal article need alignment.
        if prof['artifact_kind'] == 'journal_article':
            rec['alignment'] = 'NOT_NEEDED'
            rec['alignment_note'] = 'the bytes we hold ARE the typeset journal article'
            results.append(rec)
            continue
        if prof['artifact_kind'] == 'wrong_work':
            rec['alignment'] = 'IMPOSSIBLE_WRONG_WORK'
            rec['alignment_note'] = ('these bytes are a DIFFERENT WORK. There is nothing to align. '
                                     'The text must be removed from this row.')
            results.append(rec)
            continue

        up = unpaywall(doi)
        oa = openalex(doi)
        rec['unpaywall_outcome'] = up.get('outcome')
        rec['openalex_outcome'] = oa.get('outcome')
        rec['unpaywall_locations'] = up.get('locations')
        rec['openalex_locations'] = oa.get('locations')

        if up.get('outcome') != 'OK' and oa.get('outcome') != 'OK':
            # WE could not ask. This says NOTHING about whether a copy exists.
            rec['alignment'] = 'BACKEND_FAILED'
            rec['alignment_note'] = (f"unpaywall={up.get('outcome')} openalex={oa.get('outcome')} — "
                                     f"we could not ask. This is a fact about OUR REQUEST, not about "
                                     f"the availability of a journal version.")
            results.append(rec)
            continue

        cands = candidate_journal_locations(up, oa)
        rec['journal_version_candidates'] = cands

        if not cands:
            rec['alignment'] = 'NO_JOURNAL_BYTES'
            rec['alignment_note'] = (
                'both backends answered, and NEITHER lists an OA publishedVersion or acceptedVersion. '
                'The only OA copy is the submitted/working version we already hold. '
                'DISCOVERY LEAD ONLY — every span stays inadmissible for a journal-only answer.')
            rec['spans_verified'] = 0
            rec['spans_inadmissible'] = ncards
            results.append(rec)
            continue

        # We have a candidate. Go and get the bytes and TEST THE SPANS.
        best = None
        for c in cands:
            o, txt = fetch_doc(c['url'])
            got = dict(**c, fetch_outcome=o, words=len(txt.split()))
            if o != 'OK' or len(txt.split()) < 1200:
                got['usable'] = False
                got['why'] = (f'fetch={o}' if o != 'OK'
                              else f'{len(txt.split())} words — a stub, not the article')
                rec.setdefault('fetch_attempts', []).append(got)
                continue
            got['usable'] = True
            rec.setdefault('fetch_attempts', []).append(got)
            best = (c, txt)
            break

        if not best:
            rec['alignment'] = 'JOURNAL_BYTES_UNREACHABLE'
            rec['alignment_note'] = ('a journal version is LISTED but we could not retrieve usable '
                                     'bytes for it. Spans stay inadmissible until we can.')
            rec['spans_verified'] = 0
            rec['spans_inadmissible'] = ncards
            results.append(rec)
            continue

        cand, jtext = best
        jw = Work(id='w', title=r.get('title') or '', authors=r.get('authors') or [],
                  year=r.get('year'), venue=r.get('venue') or '', doi=doi)
        jprof = profile(jtext, jw, r.get('abstract') or '')
        rec['journal_bytes'] = dict(url=cand['url'], via=cand['via'], version=cand['version'],
                                    words=len(jtext.split()),
                                    artifact_kind=jprof['artifact_kind'],
                                    kind_basis=jprof['artifact_kind_basis'],
                                    identity=jprof['identity']['verdict'],
                                    sha256=hashlib.sha256(jtext.encode()).hexdigest())

        # The repository SAYS publishedVersion. Do the bytes agree? Re-derive; never trust the label.
        if jprof['identity']['verdict'] == 'CONTRADICTED':
            rec['alignment'] = 'JOURNAL_BYTES_WRONG_WORK'
            rec['alignment_note'] = (f"the location labelled {cand['version']} returned a DIFFERENT "
                                     f"WORK: {jprof['identity']['basis']}")
            rec['spans_verified'] = 0
            rec['spans_inadmissible'] = ncards
            results.append(rec)
            continue

        v = verify_spans(spans_by_doi.get(doi, []), jtext)
        rec['spans_verified'] = v['n_verified']
        rec['spans_inadmissible'] = v['n_unverified']
        rec['unverified_examples'] = [s[:160] for s in v['unverified'][:5]]
        if ncards == 0:
            rec['alignment'] = 'JOURNAL_BYTES_HELD_NO_SPANS'
            rec['alignment_note'] = ('we now hold the journal version, but this row mined no spans. '
                                     'The row is re-minable AGAINST THE JOURNAL BYTES.')
        elif v['n_verified'] and not v['n_unverified']:
            rec['alignment'] = 'ALIGNED_FULL'
            rec['alignment_note'] = (f"all {v['n_verified']} spans appear VERBATIM in the "
                                     f"{cand['version']}. Edge {cand['edge']} may be ASSERTED.")
        elif v['n_verified']:
            rec['alignment'] = 'ALIGNED_PARTIAL'
            rec['alignment_note'] = (
                f"{v['n_verified']} of {ncards} spans appear verbatim in the {cand['version']}; "
                f"{v['n_unverified']} DO NOT and stay inadmissible. Per-span, never per-paper.")
        else:
            rec['alignment'] = 'NOT_ALIGNED'
            rec['alignment_note'] = (
                f"we HOLD the {cand['version']} and NOT ONE of the {ncards} mined spans appears in "
                f"it. This is the risk Sol named, observed: the working paper's text is not the "
                f"journal's text.")
        results.append(rec)

    OUT.write_text(json.dumps(results, indent=1))
    print(f'wrote {OUT}  ({len(results)} rows)')
    return 0


if __name__ == '__main__':
    raise SystemExit(main())

294	def identity_agreement(text: str, work: Work, independent_abstract: str = '',
295	                       readable: bool = True) -> dict:
296	    """Do these bytes belong to the Work the row claims? THE CHECK THAT CATCHES A DIFFERENT PAPER.
297	
298	    Not one component asked this. A title matcher matched the phrase "Rise of the Machines" and
299	    handed us a MATHEMATICS PREPRINT; every downstream check then verified, correctly and
300	    irrelevantly, that the span was verbatim IN THE MATHEMATICS PREPRINT.
301	
302	    CONTRADICTED REQUIRES POSITIVE EVIDENCE OF A DIFFERENT WORK. It is never inferred from the
303	    ABSENCE of ours. My first version of this function inferred it from absence -- "none of the
304	    claimed authors appears in the text" -- and it convicted EIGHT papers, all innocent: seven were
305	    bare abstracts that simply do not carry an author block, and the eighth was Autor (2003), whose
306	    title page is (cid:N) font codes. Meanwhile the ONE genuinely foreign paper scored CONFIRMED,
307	    because its two-word title ("Rise of the Machines") appears verbatim in the maths paper.
308	    Absence of our evidence, read as evidence of absence, is the HTTP-429-means-"no free copy exists"
309	    error exactly. So the only route to CONTRADICTED is a positive, two-sided content test:
310	
311	        the row carries an abstract FROM THE BIBLIOGRAPHY (independent of whatever we fetched);
312	        the body we fetched does NOT contain that abstract's distinctive vocabulary;
313	        therefore the body is not a rendering of this work.
314	    """
315	    head = text[:20000]
316	    authors = [a for a in (work.authors or []) if len(a) > 2]
317	    named = [a for a in authors if re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(a) + r'\b', head, re.I)]
318	    twords = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{5,}', (work.title or '').lower())}
319	    tfound = {w for w in twords if w in head.lower()}
320	    # A title of one or two distinctive words is not a test: "Rise of the Machines" yields {machines},
321	    # which the maths paper contains. Below three, the title signal is UNUSABLE, not passing.
322	    title_usable = len(twords) >= 3
323	
324	    overlap: float | None = None
325	    vocab = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{5,}', (independent_abstract or '').lower())
326	             if w not in _FUNCTION_WORDS}
327	    if (len((independent_abstract or '').split()) >= _IDENT_MIN_ABSTRACT_WORDS
328	            and len(vocab) >= _IDENT_MIN_VOCAB
329	            and len(text.split()) >= _IDENT_MIN_BODY_WORDS):
330	        low = text.lower()
331	        overlap = round(sum(1 for w in vocab if w in low) / len(vocab), 3)
332	
333	    if not readable:
334	        # You cannot judge identity from bytes you cannot read. Autor (2003)'s authors and title are
335	        # absent because they are (cid:11)(cid:3)... -- that is a fact about the FONT, not the paper.
336	        verdict, basis = 'UNRESOLVED', 'bytes are not readable — identity cannot be judged'
337	    elif overlap is not None and overlap < CONTRADICTED_VOCAB_OVERLAP:
338	        verdict = 'CONTRADICTED'
339	        basis = (f'the body contains only {overlap:.0%} of the distinctive vocabulary of this work’s '
340	                 f'own abstract (genuine bodies on this corpus: 97.5–100%) — THESE ARE NOT ITS BYTES')
341	    elif named:
342	        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'{len(named)}/{len(authors)} claimed authors named in the text'
343	    elif overlap is not None and overlap >= 0.90:
344	        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'body contains {overlap:.0%} of its own abstract’s vocabulary'
345	    elif title_usable and len(tfound) >= 0.8 * len(twords):
346	        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'{len(tfound)}/{len(twords)} distinctive title words present'
347	    else:
348	        verdict, basis = 'UNRESOLVED', 'no positive evidence either way (absence proves nothing)'
349	
350	    return dict(verdict=verdict, basis=basis,
351	                authors_named=len(named), authors_claimed=len(authors),
352	                title_words_found=len(tfound), title_words=len(twords), title_usable=title_usable,
353	                abstract_vocab_overlap=overlap)

content.text:
These results need auditing against my own Law. Two of my labels may be asserting more than their content supports:

1. **`NO_JOURNAL_BYTES`'s canned note** claims "the only OA copy is the submitted version we already hold" — but for some rows the backends may have listed *nothing at all*. That's a different fact.
2. **The `ALIGNED_*` rows are a circularity risk**: if the "journal version" I fetched is the *same document already in the corpus*, then finding spans in it proves nothing — I'd be verifying text against itself.

Let me test the circularity directly.

input.command:
cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel && python3 -c "
import json,re,hashlib
R=json.load(open('outputs/version_alignment.json'))
rows=json.load(open('outputs/journal_corpus_content.json'))
def nz(s): return re.sub(r'\s+',' ',s or '').strip()
def shingles(t,k=8):
    w=nz(t).lower().split()
    return {' '.join(w[i:i+k]) for i in range(0,max(0,len(w)-k),3)}
import pathlib,hashlib as H
CACHE=pathlib.Path('cache/version_align')
print('=== CIRCULARITY AUDIT: is the fetched \"journal version\" the SAME DOCUMENT we already hold? ===')
print()
for r in R:
    jb=r.get('journal_bytes')
    if not jb: continue
    held=rows[r['idx']].get('fulltext') or ''
    key=CACHE/(H.sha256(jb['url'].encode()).hexdigest()[:20]+'.txt')
    jt=key.read_text() if key.exists() else ''
    sh_h, sh_j = shingles(held), shingles(jt)
    inter=len(sh_h&sh_j); jac=inter/max(1,len(sh_h|sh_j))
    cover = inter/max(1,len(sh_h))
    same = hashlib.sha256(nz(held).encode()).hexdigest()==hashlib.sha256(nz(jt).encode()).hexdigest()
    print(f\"idx {r['idx']:2d} {r['doi']}\")
    print(f\"   alignment={r['alignment']}  version={jb['version']}  via={jb['via']}\")
    print(f\"   held {len(held.split()):6d}w   fetched {jb['words']:6d}w\")
    print(f\"   URL fetched: {jb['url'][:100]}\")
    print(f\"   IDENTICAL after normalisation: {same}\")
    print(f\"   8-gram Jaccard(held, fetched) = {jac:.3f}   |  fraction of HELD shingles found in FETCHED = {cover:.3f}\")
    verdict = 'CIRCULAR — same document, alignment proves NOTHING' if cover>0.85 else ('DISTINCT documents' if cover<0.5 else 'OVERLAPPING — inspect')
    print(f\"   ==> {verdict}\")
    print()
"

input.command:
cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel && python3 -c "
import json,re
rows=json.load(open('outputs/journal_corpus_content.json'))
cards=json.load(open('outputs/evidence_cards_v2.json'))
from collections import Counter
nc=Counter(c['doi'] for c in cards)

def typeset_evidence(ft, venue):
    ev={}
    # 1. running heads: the journal's name repeated on page furniture (>=2 occurrences)
    vn = re.sub(r'&','&',venue or '')
    core = [w for w in re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{4,}', vn) if w.lower() not in ('the','and','of','for')]
    if core:
        pat = r'\b' + r'\W+'.join(re.escape(w) for w in core[:4]) + r'\b'
        ev['journal_name_in_text'] = len(re.findall(pat, ft, re.I))
    else:
        ev['journal_name_in_text']=0
    # 2. form-feed page breaks immediately followed by a page number = printed folios
    ev['formfeed_folios'] = len(re.findall(r'\x0c\s*\d{1,4}\b', ft))
    ev['formfeeds'] = ft.count('\x0c')
    # 3. explicit working-paper series marks
    ev['wp_marks'] = len(re.findall(r'(nber working paper|working paper (no|series)|discussion paper|this draft|preliminary)', ft[:6000], re.I))
    return ev

print(f\"{'idx':>3} {'cards':>5}  {'venue':28s} {'jname_hits':>10} {'folios':>6} {'wp_marks':>8}  verdict\")
print('-'*118)
for i,r in enumerate(rows):
    ft=r.get('fulltext') or ''
    if len(ft.split())<400: continue
    ev=typeset_evidence(ft, r.get('venue'))
    n=nc.get(r['doi'],0)
    # publisher typeset => journal name appears as a RUNNING HEAD (many times) AND printed folios
    typeset = ev['journal_name_in_text']>=3 and ev['formfeed_folios']>=3

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""THE ALIGNMENT CENSUS — which held bytes may name a journal article, and which may not.

Every label here is RE-DERIVED FROM THE BYTES. Three checks in this file exist because the first
version of it produced a confident, wrong answer, and its own audit caught it:

 1. THE CIRCULARITY GUARD.  My first pass reported three papers "ALIGNED — all spans verified in the
    publishedVersion". They were not aligned. Unpaywall's `publishedVersion` URL pointed at THE SAME
    SPRINGER PDF ALREADY IN THE CORPUS, and I had verified the text against itself. An alignment is
    only an alignment if the journal bytes are a DIFFERENT DOCUMENT from the bytes we hold, so the
    fetched text must now clear an 8-gram distinctness test before any span is counted.

 2. THE PUBLISHER-TYPESET TEST.  `_JOURNAL_MARK` in provenance.py matches a DOI string — but a DOI
    string is printed by every repository COVER SHEET, which cites the article rather than being it.
    The article of record is identified by furniture only a typesetter emits: the journal's name
    recurring as a RUNNING HEAD, and printed page FOLIOS after form-feeds, matching the bibliographic
    page range. That test, and not the metadata, is what proved the LSE deposit of Goos-Manning-
    Salomons really is the AER article (running heads "AMERICAN ECONOMIC REVIEW August 2014", folios
    2509-2526) -- against Unpaywall, which labels that same file `submittedVersion`.

 3. BACKEND_FAILED IS NEVER "NO COPY".  HTTP 403 from AEA and SAGE is a fact about our request.
    It is recorded as ACCESS_BLOCKED and it concludes NOTHING about what exists.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import re
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path('/home/polaris/wt/flywheel')


def nz(s: str) -> str:
    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s or '').strip()


def shingles(t: str, k: int = 8) -> set:
    w = nz(t).lower().split()
    return {' '.join(w[i:i + k]) for i in range(0, max(0, len(w) - k), 3)}


def venue_running_head_pattern(venue: str):
    toks = re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', re.sub(r'&', '&', venue or ''))
    if not toks:
        return None
    return r'\b' + r'[\s\W]+(?:of|and|the|in|for)?[\s\W]*'.join(re.escape(t) for t in toks[:4]) + r'\b'


def typeset_profile(text: str, venue: str) -> dict:
    """Is this the PUBLISHER'S rendering? Only a typesetter emits running heads and printed folios."""
    p = venue_running_head_pattern(venue)
    return dict(
        running_heads=len(re.findall(p, text, re.I)) if p else 0,
        folios=len(re.findall(r'\x0c\s*\d{1,4}\b', text)),
        wp_series_marks=len(re.findall(
            r'(nber working paper|working paper (no|series)|discussion paper|this draft)',
            text[:6000], re.I)),
    )


def is_publisher_typeset(tp: dict) -> bool:
    # Both, together. A cover sheet can print the journal's name once; it cannot print the article's
    # page folios across a form-feed on every page.
    return tp['running_heads'] >= 3 and tp['folios'] >= 3


def main() -> int:
    corpus = json.loads((ROOT / 'outputs' / 'journal_corpus_content.json').read_text())
    cards = json.loads((ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards_v2.json').read_text())
    align = {r['idx']: r for r in json.loads((ROOT / 'outputs' / 'version_alignment.json').read_text())}

    ncards: dict[str, int] = {}
    for c in cards:
        ncards[c['doi']] = ncards.get(c['doi'], 0) + 1

    import sys
    sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
    from provenance import profile, Work

    out = []
    for i, r in enumerate(corpus):
        ft = r.get('fulltext') or ''
        if not ft.strip():
            continue
        doi = r.get('doi') or ''
        w = Work(id='w', title=r.get('title') or '', authors=r.get('authors') or [],
                 year=r.get('year'), venue=r.get('venue') or '', doi=doi)
        prof = profile(ft, w, r.get('abstract') or '')
        tp = typeset_profile(ft, r.get('venue') or '')
        a = align.get(i, {})
        n = ncards.get(doi, 0)

        # ---- THE RULING. Derived, in the order of what the bytes can prove. --------------------
        if prof['identity']['verdict'] == 'CONTRADICTED':
            kind, ruling = 'WRONG_WORK', 'PURGE'
            why = 'these bytes are a different work by a different author — nothing to align'
        elif prof['artifact_kind'] == 'landing_page':
            kind, ruling = 'LANDING_PAGE', 'NO_EVIDENCE'
            why = 'a web page about the document, not the document'
        elif prof['extractability']['verdict'] == 'CORRUPT':
            kind, ruling = 'EXTRACTION_FAILURE', 'NO_EVIDENCE'
            why = 'bytes are not readable prose'
        elif tp['wp_series_marks'] >= 1 and not is_publisher_typeset(tp):
            kind, ruling = 'WORKING_PAPER', 'INADMISSIBLE'
            why = 'the bytes self-declare a working-paper series and carry no publisher typeset furniture'
        elif is_publisher_typeset(tp):
            kind, ruling = 'JOURNAL_ARTICLE', 'ADMISSIBLE'
            why = (f"publisher typeset furniture: {tp['running_heads']} running heads of the journal's "
                   f"name, {tp['folios']} printed page folios — only a typesetter emits these")
        elif (a.get('journal_bytes') or {}).get('version') == 'acceptedVersion':
            kind, ruling = 'ACCEPTED_MANUSCRIPT', 'ADMISSIBLE'
            why = ('an authenticated post-peer-review accepted manuscript (repository version '
                   'statement, via Unpaywall) — accepted_manuscript_of is span-preserving')
        else:
            kind, ruling = 'UNDETERMINED_VERSION', 'INADMISSIBLE'
            why = ('no publisher typeset furniture and no authenticated version statement — we cannot '
                   'show these bytes are the article of record')

        out.append(dict(
            idx=i, doi=doi, title=r.get('title'), venue=r.get('venue'), year=r.get('year'),
            authors=r.get('authors'), held_words=len(ft.split()), cards=n,
            wp_fetch_tag=r.get('fulltext_source'), corpus_status=r.get('content_status'),
            held_kind=kind, ruling=ruling, why=why, typeset=tp,

The headline estimate was cut by nearly half in peer review. And evidence card #1 currently reads `effect: "0.37 percentage points lower"`, `attribution: "Writing in the Journal of Political Economy in 2020, Acemoglu and Restrepo"`. **The artifact was one compose-run from printing 0.37 and crediting it to a journal that says 0.2.** Sol's risk is not theoretical on this corpus; it is realised, on the single most-quoted number in it.

Note the failure shape: the span *is* verbatim. The gate would have passed it. The document named is simply not the document the span came from.

### 4. Bottom line

**119 of 192 evidence cards (62%) are mined from documents that are not the journal article they are attributed to.** Only **70** sit on admissible bytes. Task 72 demands journal articles only, so all 119 must be withheld — the weaker corpus wins, exactly as Sol said. The three genuinely-admissible economics papers (JEP 2019, AER 2014, ET&P) currently contribute **zero** cards, and Autor (2015) is now re-minable from the real JEP article: that is where the recovery lies, not in the working papers.

Two further defects surfaced: **65 of 192 card spans no longer verify against the current corpus bytes at all** (cards mined 21:24, corpus overwritten 21:29 — the bytes churned underneath them), and **idx 66 has a card mined from bytes that are not readable prose** (stopword density 0.0126).

### 5. My own harness failed its first audit — reporting that too

My first pass reported three papers "ALIGNED — all spans verified in the publishedVersion." **They were not aligned.** Unpaywall's `publishedVersion` URL pointed at the same Springer PDF already in the corpus, and I had verified the text against itself (8-gram coverage 1.000 — same document). A circularity guard now requires the journal bytes to be a *distinct document* before any span counts. Same failure shape as everything else tonight: a label asserting more than its content supported.

Two labels I refused to soften: AEA and SAGE returned **HTTP 403** (a bot block). That is recorded as `ACCESS_BLOCKED` — a fact about our request — never as "no free copy." I did not spoof a browser UA to evade it.

One correction to the tooling: **Unpaywall labels the LSE deposit of Goos-Manning-Salomons `submittedVersion`, and it is wrong.** The bytes carry `American Economic Review 2014, 104(8): 2509–2526`, alternating publisher running heads, and printed page folios — furniture only a typesetter emits. The bytes overruled the metadata.
